news.summary: On May 22, 2025, Graphic Packaging Holding Company revealed that Alessandro Maselli has been appointed to its Board of Directors. Maselli, who serves as the president and CEO of Catalent, brings valuable expertise to the company. Graphic Packaging Holding Company, a prominent player in sustainable consumer packaging, trades on the NYSE under the symbol GPK. This strategic addition is expected to enhance the board's capabilities and drive further growth for the organization.
news.headline: Graphic Packaging Holding Company appoints Alessandro Maselli to its Board of Directors; CEO of Catalent.

news.summary: In San Francisco, Innovaccer Inc. revealed the debut of its Healthcare Intelligence Platform, 'Innovaccer Gravity™'. The platform aims to assist organizations in maximizing their data value and hastening AI-driven transformation. Innovaccer Gravity™ smoothly merges healthcare enterprise data, facilitates cross-domain intelligence, and yields quicker ROI, while lowering TCO. This enterprise data integration is vital for accelerating digital healthcare advancements.
news.headline: Innovaccer launches 'Innovaccer Gravity™', a Healthcare Intelligence Platform to accelerate AI-driven transformation, reducing TCO.

news.summary: A company based in Jacksonville, Florida called Redwire Corporation (NYSE: RDW), recognized for their work in space infrastructure, revealed that they recently secured a deal with SpaceTech GmbH to supply important avionics technology for the European Space Agency's (ESA) Next Generation Gravity Mission (NGGM) satellites. These satellites form part of ESA's involvement in a joint initiative with NASA for an Earth observation satellite group, titled Mass Change and Geophysics Internationa.
news.headline: Redwire wins contract for avionics tech for ESA NGGM satellites, part of NASA-ESA Earth observation constellation. #RDW

news.summary: The global CNC plasma cutting machines market was valued at USD 673.2 million in 2024 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of over 5.1% from 2025 to 2034, reaching an estimated USD 1.1 billion by 2034. Key drivers include technological advancements, rising demand across industrial applications, and infrastructure development in emerging economies. Market trends show a shift towards sustainable manufacturing and smart operations. The market is segmented by product type (gantry-type and table-type CNC plasma cutting machines) and by end-use industry (automotive, aerospace, marine, construction, manufacturing, metal industry, educational institutions, and others). The metal industry was the dominant end-user segment in 2024, accounting for approximately 31% of the total market share. Despite operational advantages like lower cutting costs and high efficiency, the market faces competition from laser cutting technologies and challenges related to high initial capital requirements, maintenance costs, and skilled labor. Fluctuations in raw material prices, particularly steel and aluminum, also impact market stability, and economic downturns can lead to decreased demand for CNC plasma cutting machines.
news.headline: CNC Plasma Cutting Machines Market to hit USD 1.1 billion by 2034, with global market valued at USD 673.2 million in 2024, growing at a CAGR of over 5.1% from 2025 to 2034. Major drivers include technological advancements, rising demand in key sectors, and infrastructure development in emerging economies. Market segmented by product type with gantry-type CNC plasma cutting machines taking the lead. Metal industry was the dominant end-user segment in 2024. Market facing competition from laser cutting technologies and impacted by volatility in raw material prices.

news.summary: Crypto awareness in Singapore is at an all-time high, with 94% familiar with digital assets, but ownership has dropped to 29% in 2024 from 40% in 2023. Men are more active investors at 35% compared to 24% for women, with millennials and Gen X dominating at 71%. 53% of current holders plan to increase their positions, while 17% of non-holders are interested in entering the market. Bitcoin is the top crypto, owned by 68% and seen as a currency, store of value, or investment asset by 86%. 77% believe Bitcoin will exceed $100,000 by 2030. Singapore is a global hub for blockchain and crypto development with 1,600 blockchain patents, 2,433 crypto jobs, and 81 exchanges. Singaporean investors also hold stablecoins (46%) and memecoins (28%), with Dogecoin being the most popular. Cryptos like Ethereum, Dogecoin, Shiba Inu, and Solana are also recognized in the Singapore market. Arbitrage trading has seen an increase, with 67% having sold holdings to benefit from price fluctuations.
news.headline: Singapore's crypto awareness hits record 94%, but ownership dips to 29% in 2024. Men more active, millennials and Gen X dominate. Source: Independent Reserve.CreatedAt: 2022-01-11

news.summary: A renowned company in electrical solutions, nVent (NYSE: NVT), is set to partake in the upcoming William Blair Growth Stock Conference on June 5, 2025, in London. The Chair and Chief Executive Officer, Beth Wozniak, will deliver a presentation at 9:20 a.m. CT on that day. Those interested can access a webcast of the event on nVent’s Investor Relations site at https://investors.nvent.com/events-and-presentations. nVent is known for its top-notch electrical connection and protection offerings, solidifying its position as a leading global provider in the industry.
news.headline: nVent Electric (NYSE: NVT) to join William Blair Growth Stock Conference on June 5, 2025, with CEO Beth Wozniak presenting. Characters: 108

news.summary: A biopharmaceutical company called InflaRx N.V. (Nasdaq: IFRX) based in JENA, Germany, revealed on May 22, 2025, that they are leading the way in developing anti-inflammatory treatments that focus on the complement system. They shared plans to take part in various investor conferences scheduled for June and July 2025.
news.headline: InflaRx (Nasdaq: IFRX) will attend investor conferences in June and July 2025 to showcase anti-inflammatory therapeutics.

news.summary: Honeywell International Inc. has announced its acquisition of Johnson Matthey's Catalyst Technologies division in an all-cash transaction valued at 1.8 billion pounds ($2.4 billion). The acquisition price equates to approximately 11 times the estimated 2025 EBITDA, factoring in tax benefits and expected cost synergies. Through the integration of Catalyst Technologies into Honeywell's Energy and Sustainability Solutions (ESS) segment, the company anticipates strengthening its portfolio with lucrative high-growth prospects and achieving significant additional cost savings. This acquisition aligns well with Honeywell's UOP operations, focusing on catalysts and process technologies, and is projected to expand Honeywell's presence in the market. This move comes after the recent acquisition of Sundyne for $2.16 billion, further expanding Honeywell's Energy and Industrial Equipment offerings.
news.headline: Honeywell to acquire Johnson Matthey Catalyst Technologies for $2.4 billion, enhancing portfolio and cost efficiencies.

news.summary: Quoin Pharmaceuticals Ltd., a specialty pharmaceutical company, has received FDA approval to begin a second pivotal clinical trial, CL-QRX003-002, for their lead product QRX003, a topical lotion designed to treat Netherton Syndrome (NS). This rare disease is characterized by excessive skin shedding due to hyperactivity of kallikreins in the skin. Data from Quoin's studies shows rapid and prolonged skin healing with twice-daily use of QRX003, effectively reducing symptoms like chronic itching and improving sleep quality. The company is focused on rare and orphan diseases, and their NASDAQ ticker symbol is QNRX.
news.headline: Quoin Pharma gets FDA clearance for second pivotal QRX003 Netherton Syndrome clinical study, showing skin healing with a topical lotion.

news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on May 22, 2025, Almaden Minerals Ltd. (TSX: AMM; OTCQB: AAUAF) has informed Almadex Minerals Ltd. of its decision to make an early repayment on the gold loan as per the secured gold loan agreement. The loan, initially disclosed through press releases on June 27, 2024, and May 14, 2019, is now being settled ahead of schedule. This transaction marks a significant financial move for the companies involved.
news.headline: Almaden Minerals Ltd repays gold loan to Almadex Minerals Ltd early; agreement was amended. TSX: AMM; OTCQB: AAUAF.

news.summary: Vigil Neuroscience, Inc. announced a deal with Sanofi for acquisition at an equity value of around $470 million. Sanofi will pay $8.00 per share in cash for Vigil and shareholders may receive an additional $2.00 per share through a non-tradeable contingent value right (CVR) if VG-3927 achieves its first commercial sale within a specified period. The total equity value of the transaction, including the potential CVR payment, is estimated at about $600 million. The acquisition is projected to conclude in the third quarter. Additionally, Vigil Neuroscience's Alzheimer's candidate has shown a promising safety profile, with plans for a mid-stage study to commence in Q3.
news.headline: Sanofi to acquire Vigil Neuroscience for $470 million in equity, with additional $130 million potential.

news.summary: In an age where the cost of childcare is prohibitively high for numerous families, a fresh report reveals that directors are also facing challenges as they are overwhelmed with time-consuming tasks such as reporting, scheduling, and communication. The study, released in ARLINGTON, Va. on May 22, 2025, highlights the struggle faced by families who cannot afford proper care and also sheds light on the pressure directors face due to relentless administrative responsibilities. This situation underscores the urgency of addressing the childcare crisis, with X% of families unable to access affordable care, while directors are grappling with managing tasks on a daily basis.
news.headline: Childcare leaders face stress and overtime crisis, struggling with reporting, scheduling, and communication tasks.
